LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NORMA GREAVER'S MOTION FOR ISSUANCE OF PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH A REASONABLE PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFF MERELY ALLEGES THAT DECEDENT AGREED TO CARE FOR HER. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT SHE IS ENTITLED TO ANY TRUST PROPERTY. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH IRREPARABLE HARM, OR THAT MONEY DAMAGES ARE INADEQUATE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Advance Trial Date [Ccp 36(A)]
Off Calendar Mar-05-2009 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction.
Off Calendar Feb-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF NORMA GREAVER'S Motion For Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Timothy M. Flaherty, Carroll, Burdick & McDonough LLP (415) 989-5900, appearing for Defendant, and opposing party; Geoffrey Spellberg, of the Spellberg Law Offices; representing Plaintiff and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for preliminary injunction is Denied. Plaintiff fails to establish a reasonable probability of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff merely alleges that decedent agreed to care for her. Insufficient evidence that she is entitled to any trust property. Plaintiff fails to establish irreparable harm, or that money damages are inadequate. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Issuance Of Preliminary Injunction.
